| Why this tiny German hearing aid is taking the U.S. by storm, according to the experts
---
[ ](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=a6c24c9416984d1d8ddf26b046687562&_e=BzL4FcOEfGWELvB49hQbP6q0l7WqStzoTUKRH-ZoJxSJSems3g9bu6FvKbCsLYkXsLTIdlqjPPx6TvQSMcg_WnaOEZ5MvBRsk6VzXD03KK5jNnxxI-vv-HgWaG6ZyGIjgkONvWUaEm3un4BaDGtNkX3IZCX_cAEPCmqv5aybhz78M-tPGehflMyWoO-ZWI0Nw8EXrQ5YS4LLdxzSoZD55_88y3sRjLNQ8VmvopxM5RQaPoeplB17zX-mTbLwsZbTJc1raSqyROqBhTI5p_6QaIDvgta1SM7n-E3nOeUUIemxmMyM2drZyVCr1kLNN_RRXjXnCQX3tkn6fyZY2RFzN-GdQidKNDNNKhp1uAz07m21Xz8r-kVQRV9L2YyQrCOFEzr0Z_YmZcu9MfEjKf2ImlA5VpYhgk05jLKlC9zRGksabS5jt8XHYeAPPBHKO-8LFc7-jD53-03iQK_KLIefOw%3D%3D)
**Alarming fact: More than 48 million Americans hear so poorly that their quality of life significantly suffers as a result.**

The problem: Most wait too long to act, hoping their hearing will improve on its own. Sadly, it never does.

But now, **a game-changing device is revolutionizing the hearing aid industry,** and experts say it's the biggest breakthrough they've seen in over a decade.

It's [hear.com](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=a6c24c9416984d1d8ddf26b046687562&_e=BzL4FcOEfGWELvB49hQbP6q0l7WqStzoTUKRH-ZoJxSJSems3g9bu6FvKbCsLYkXsLTIdlqjPPx6TvQSMcg_WnaOEZ5MvBRsk6VzXD03KK5jNnxxI-vv-HgWaG6ZyGIjgkONvWUaEm3un4BaDGtNkX3IZCX_cAEPCmqv5aybhz78M-tPGehflMyWoO-ZWI0Nw8EXrQ5YS4LLdxzSoZD55_88y3sRjLNQ8VmvopxM5RQaPoeplB17zX-mTbLwsZbTJc1raSqyROqBhTI5p_6QaIDvgta1SM7n-E3nOeUUIemxmMyM2drZyVCr1kLNN_RRUFFZ_mEZxJ6EUNZ7POat5YGvVEHBrnPIgkFlK8wBlPUB8pHxEZQSZdHcns7WaFbe-ZrTqx9gMJ43GLbbhFfrBaJoJfy6EJ7z8qsBlxZecR-fC25yL6_LWqzpLCT8Re74Xm0QctGmLg2wyyKS2cSTvA%3D%3D)'s **new IX hearing aid.**

And it’s the perfect solution at the perfect time—when more and more people are eager to maintain their quality of life and overcome these limitations.

It’s no wonder **[hear.com](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=a6c24c9416984d1d8ddf26b046687562&_e=BzL4FcOEfGWELvB49hQbP6q0l7WqStzoTUKRH-ZoJxSJSems3g9bu6FvKbCsLYkXsLTIdlqjPPx6TvQSMcg_WnaOEZ5MvBRsk6VzXD03KK5jNnxxI-vv-HgWaG6ZyGIjgkONvWUaEm3un4BaDGtNkX3IZCX_cAEPCmqv5aybhz78M-tPGehflMyWoO-ZWI0Nw8EXrQ5YS4LLdxzSoZD55_88y3sRjLNQ8VmvopxM5RQaPoeplB17zX-mTbLwsZbTJc1raSqyROqBhTI5p_6QaIDvgta1SM7n-E3nOeUUIemxmMyM2drZyVCr1kLNN_RR13t-0yDbp4Oa03viprKqWd4Znl3ZB-FB01ZEdgrmlu3GRSQlWoc1ej2mODwYOttgA1fCV9Bg-KUoARl2w7nN8gTf5KfDnKFOJ5LIFvW2u9S1nD3lpfq4g7e-UqZ0FckTNXfuGnAOTboMHL8Xl7z7CQ%3D%3D)'s IX hearing aid has taken the U.S. by storm since its release earlier this year** offering hope and unprecedented clarity to thousands across America.
[ Check if you qualify ](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=a6c24c9416984d1d8ddf26b046687562&_e=BzL4FcOEfGWELvB49hQbP6q0l7WqStzoTUKRH-ZoJxSJSems3g9bu6FvKbCsLYkXsLTIdlqjPPx6TvQSMcg_WnaOEZ5MvBRsk6VzXD03KK5jNnxxI-vv-HgWaG6ZyGIjgkONvWUaEm3un4BaDGtNkX3IZCX_cAEPCmqv5aybhz78M-tPGehflMyWoO-ZWI0Nw8EXrQ5YS4LLdxzSoZD55_88y3sRjLNQ8VmvopxM5RQaPoeplB17zX-mTbLwsZbTJc1raSqyROqBhTI5p_6QaIDvgta1SM7n-E3nOeUUIemxmMyM2drZyVCr1kLNN_RR_p9WJ8Kn0tJjZD9Lp8-VSYHvT8JbGLvLW8JqlxN0TdU0zhAwGrIOol3bss9VyKh_bB1V7TlEFRPYVnP6Huer4DALczuGb7FkYeQUiXow6CimnL7tMAJ4XFMAvz90e7kttMMb8TS0GC3rmrhPjEltMw%3D%3D)
[ ](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=a6c24c9416984d1d8ddf26b046687562&_e=BzL4FcOEfGWELvB49hQbP6q0l7WqStzoTUKRH-ZoJxSJSems3g9bu6FvKbCsLYkXsLTIdlqjPPx6TvQSMcg_WnaOEZ5MvBRsk6VzXD03KK5jNnxxI-vv-HgWaG6ZyGIjgkONvWUaEm3un4BaDGtNkX3IZCX_cAEPCmqv5aybhz78M-tPGehflMyWoO-ZWI0Nw8EXrQ5YS4LLdxzSoZD55_88y3sRjLNQ8VmvopxM5RQaPoeplB17zX-mTbLwsZbTJc1raSqyROqBhTI5p_6QaIDvgta1SM7n-E3nOeUUIemxmMyM2drZyVCr1kLNN_RRhMr3-RZoBORGSdK0HiB4qrEZwvDCgvaNm_Yd5OMjeAvpIpVhaWkTLjD1wm2P4OOAbBJZLABsivgbeY-gqNgipg-ikKuP_EX-koWzMW9rsKtCUFK6KyUcWFiyUnzGUDIhfqHnJP8zRe75YrhKcee54w%3D%3D)
What makes [hear.com](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=a6c24c9416984d1d8ddf26b046687562&_e=BzL4FcOEfGWELvB49hQbP6q0l7WqStzoTUKRH-ZoJxSJSems3g9bu6FvKbCsLYkXsLTIdlqjPPx6TvQSMcg_WnaOEZ5MvBRsk6VzXD03KK5jNnxxI-vv-HgWaG6ZyGIjgkONvWUaEm3un4BaDGtNkX3IZCX_cAEPCmqv5aybhz78M-tPGehflMyWoO-ZWI0Nw8EXrQ5YS4LLdxzSoZD55_88y3sRjLNQ8VmvopxM5RQaPoeplB17zX-mTbLwsZbTJc1raSqyROqBhTI5p_6QaIDvgta1SM7n-E3nOeUUIemxmMyM2drZyVCr1kLNN_RR6RO_rlbLxzLvHll8mTc8Z8BYfwv8xeBwUXfzMwrDNcmXUUIl7_mnVGdSP8lo42oPKnORc-hncO_M_ydncRusifZmA9KpyUt3eNW_iy53Q8C9qTxyoQfVAs3HiGjgN1lBgwnh9yeWB3bfwfLSG8oQkA%3D%3D)’s IX hearing aid so special?

It’s one of **the world’s first hearing aids with dual processing.**

This is special because it’s the first time engineers have been able to pack not one, but two state-of-the-art computer chips into a device this small.

The result? **An unprecedented amount of power and clarity.**

This means that [hear.com](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=a6c24c9416984d1d8ddf26b046687562&_e=BzL4FcOEfGWELvB49hQbP6q0l7WqStzoTUKRH-ZoJxSJSems3g9bu6FvKbCsLYkXsLTIdlqjPPx6TvQSMcg_WnaOEZ5MvBRsk6VzXD03KK5jNnxxI-vv-HgWaG6ZyGIjgkONvWUaEm3un4BaDGtNkX3IZCX_cAEPCmqv5aybhz78M-tPGehflMyWoO-ZWI0Nw8EXrQ5YS4LLdxzSoZD55_88y3sRjLNQ8VmvopxM5RQaPoeplB17zX-mTbLwsZbTJc1raSqyROqBhTI5p_6QaIDvgta1SM7n-E3nOeUUIemxmMyM2drZyVCr1kLNN_RRcjFz2Ex_RgbKoOwGXbIqY9XcU9m9NWBeFxwOqspDCON-yl_1KgcJYvboOW4_GWP16RqotXOeNHkcc9n18GOE-OYEJUJLTzDMt2tToJ8daUe4g9z0YCEkugwFTu0Z3FueZ36WfK2bL5vCOUI4OzmmbQ%3D%3D)’s IX hearing aids offer all of the usual benefits of modern hearing aids—from smartphone connectivity to Bluetooth streaming—with the added bonus of something completely new: they can process speech and background noise independently, then recombine them for **unparalleled, crystal clear sound.**

“It's not just about volume,” explains [hear.com](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=a6c24c9416984d1d8ddf26b046687562&_e=BzL4FcOEfGWELvB49hQbP6q0l7WqStzoTUKRH-ZoJxSJSems3g9bu6FvKbCsLYkXsLTIdlqjPPx6TvQSMcg_WnaOEZ5MvBRsk6VzXD03KK5jNnxxI-vv-HgWaG6ZyGIjgkONvWUaEm3un4BaDGtNkX3IZCX_cAEPCmqv5aybhz78M-tPGehflMyWoO-ZWI0Nw8EXrQ5YS4LLdxzSoZD55_88y3sRjLNQ8VmvopxM5RQaPoeplB17zX-mTbLwsZbTJc1raSqyROqBhTI5p_6QaIDvgta1SM7n-E3nOeUUIemxmMyM2drZyVCr1kLNN_RRE-5WWK5hpY_a6sn-8sTZEb6ttLYwXjEBHNZW8QkPBc-Ob8IGFmalzGPomlqaK4S49QVOusDPe1KnyB3eyDDh21lyY0rkMlWW6oEGmZr_Yxmi2jQ-llmJ1ytw_e5aiyrsDf1wVGlFPCNVGHWwAqgwzA%3D%3D) founder Paul Crusius. “It's about speech clarity. And that's precisely what IX is designed to optimize.”

In other words: **with double the power comes double the clarity—and all that in a virtually invisible design!**

Limited no-risk trials now available

Click below to check if you qualify for a 45-day no-risk trial. As the Germans always say: "there's never been a better time." So do yourself a favor and don’t wait. **Get started today.**
[ Check if you qualify ](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=a6c24c9416984d1d8ddf26b046687562&_e=BzL4FcOEfGWELvB49hQbP6q0l7WqStzoTUKRH-ZoJxSJSems3g9bu6FvKbCsLYkXsLTIdlqjPPx6TvQSMcg_WnaOEZ5MvBRsk6VzXD03KK5jNnxxI-vv-HgWaG6ZyGIjgkONvWUaEm3un4BaDGtNkX3IZCX_cAEPCmqv5aybhz78M-tPGehflMyWoO-ZWI0Nw8EXrQ5YS4LLdxzSoZD55_88y3sRjLNQ8VmvopxM5RQaPoeplB17zX-mTbLwsZbTJc1raSqyROqBhTI5p_6QaIDvgta1SM7n-E3nOeUUIemxmMyM2drZyVCr1kLNN_RRjbkbeiA9VsQekjSPB996rdQKbuml9R151RQvGY_xC03JZ83dKZqsrdiA_MbGa_mMnsxXGLZ32M9FgyS0oD4nZTxBcKeXTcpx0dpr2IgJH7YE-HOwr6ldI0sMbgM019m5A3WB2mefLMS4vgKUOlc0Lg%3D%3D)

[Unsubscribe](http://links.g4yamerica.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_s=a6c24c9416984d1d8ddf26b046687562&_t=twWG5BNPjoLolk0adUqeEjkzzVwlI8Kj464eFncOayzun4BAThGX2z8zdCEXpk4o9NzVc5to80UjLzJ5GL0rkRxn2kcDoeq4kamUKCYKmreWjSotLOA75GdvdWBXzCZc)

# [How America Actually Wins](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=159128439&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU5MTI4NDM5LCJpYXQiOjE3NDIwNTMyMTYsImV4cCI6MTc0NDY0NTIxNiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.tV9Z4NPMmIZ1C0IWuKNfgNCDfAW6dxTH1uB2WpwuPDE)
### The National Security Case for the Next Generation of Energy
| | [Lucas Kunce](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)
---
| Mar 15
---
| [](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=159128439&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU5MTI4NDM5LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0MjA1MzIxNiwiZXhwIjoxNzQ0NjQ1MjE2LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTg2Nzc0MSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.mZ40AvNlVjphO8M_tb1sqrA3-pO8m991IXo7mKyVdco&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=159128439&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU5MTI4NDM5LCJpYXQiOjE3NDIwNTMyMTYsImV4cCI6MTc0NDY0NTIxNiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.tV9Z4NPMmIZ1C0IWuKNfgNCDfAW6dxTH1uB2WpwuPDE&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=159128439&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU5MTI4NDM5LCJpYXQiOjE3NDIwNTMyMTYsImV4cCI6MTc0NDY0NTIxNiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.tV9Z4NPMmIZ1C0IWuKNfgNCDfAW6dxTH1uB2WpwuPDE)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvbHVjYXNrdW5jZS9wL2hvdy1hbWVyaWNhLWFjdHVhbGx5LXdpbnM_._MzcCp-vg-ssJm3_LIGX_evD3Y95iLoWHDpW_9kI0jI?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/lucaskunce/p/how-america-actually-wins?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Looks like someone high up read [last week's post about Russia-Ukraine](https://substack.com/redirect/119fe67d-106a-42e6-9fd1-b38b49b0ad6e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)! Because the US thankfully reversed course on the critical negotiating error of unilaterally giving up leverage like aid and intel sharing when dealing Russian realpolitik.
Another thing I talked about, however, that has not been addressed, is how Europe made this conflict possible by buying what it thought was "cheap" natural gas from Russia for years. Gas that seemed cheap because the sticker price didn't include other external costs, like the fact that the money was going to fuel Russia's war machine which Europe is now having to spend a lot of money counteracting. In the long run, that "cheap" natural gas is going to cost significantly more than if they had invested in that money in the next generation of energy.
America has been making the same mistake for decades, thinking that fossil fuels are cheap because we only look at the sticker price. It's time all of us doubled down on making the case that decarbonization and the transition to the next generation of energy is _the_ critical ingredient of future American security and strength.
I first [made the case for that in the American Prospect](https://substack.com/redirect/84bebe01-57a2-4a09-8782-e05e2e4e64a1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), this is how it's done.
Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.-Mf-6YIbtucU02-lbp5j5h7dFtWkSrCYOWt67Hu9THA?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=159128439)
I spent a decent chunk of 2009 rolling down the roads of western Iraq, working with Iraqi police, and trying not to get blown up. A few years ago, on my birthday, I tried to reflect back on the birthday I spent there near the end of that deployment. How different it was. But, looking back, I couldn't even tell you where I went that day. Fallujah? Ramadi? Habbaniyah? It's hard to tell. It's been more than a decade since most of us were there and, while some things are still quite sharp, the day-to-day stuff is getting fuzzy.
I did remember a running joke that would come up now and again, though, because it was exactly the type of thing that would come up on a birthday in Iraq. Occasionally, when the opportunity seemed right, someone would wonder aloud, "Why are we here again?" And the answer, delivered dryly, was always some refrain of: "Oh, yeah. Oil."
It's not like we were being clever. Everyone knew it. The old CENTCOM commander, John Abizaid, had said it quite clearly in 2007: "Of course it's about oil; we can't really deny that." Alan Greenspan had agreed, and Chuck Hagel directly confirmed it: "People say we're not fighting for oil. Of course we are."
It's the reason we've cared about the Middle East for decades. It's almost certainly the reason I spent parts of 2012, 2013, and 2014 in Afghanistan, too. Al-Qaeda would have likely never existed were it not for the U.S. obsession with oil, considering that the [chief popular complaints](https://substack.com/redirect/3fff2d44-2564-4347-ba75-418076372585?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that al-Qaeda exploited centered around our actions in the Middle East. Without al-Qaeda, we would have cared about Afghanistan and the Taliban about the same amount we care about Eritrea and its repressive king. Which, of course, is not at all.
Without an oil addiction, the U.S. military could avoid a number of costly and resource-intensive still-ongoing operations in the area, such as working the Fifth Fleet overtime to keep the [Strait of Hormuz](https://substack.com/redirect/f6df9310-5914-4c86-ab5d-835cf3f1bd49?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) open. In 2019, Secretary of State Mike Pompeo affirmed that the U.S. would keep the straits [open at any cost militarily](https://substack.com/redirect/ba595e9f-6720-4eef-a6ad-46fa351adff6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and that policy has never wavered, regardless of the President. Which is an incredible amount of dedication for an expensive policy that, ironically, helps our adversary, China, more than us. [Seventy-six percent of the oil flowing through those straits in 2018 went to Asia](https://substack.com/redirect/f6df9310-5914-4c86-ab5d-835cf3f1bd49?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), not America. And China received more than double the amount that came here.
As my generation enters middle age and we look back at the several decades of conflict we endured because of oil, and the effort we continue to put toward it, it's obvious that it has never been worth the cost. The time. The lives. The dollars. The price tag on U.S. actions in Iraq, Afghanistan, Pakistan, and Syria in the 21st century totals [more than $14 trillion](https://substack.com/redirect/b31f3ad2-4f17-481d-8261-3e765437912a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Not to mention the intangible cost of allying with countries like Saudi Arabia, incubator of 15 of the 19 9/11 hijackers. The country that [funded and exported](https://substack.com/redirect/1851c799-8d6f-4440-b3ad-eca9232c6cc0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [Wahhabi extremism](https://substack.com/redirect/2b49fe66-2c3c-4e56-84bd-807f5b86415e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and anti-American sentiment for decades. The [fourth-most](https://substack.com/redirect/835755c1-ae17-4a49-ab42-2fbe85c530c5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) repressive country in the world that [murders journalists](https://substack.com/redirect/19d4989c-3593-4ceb-a35e-3c663afe9ecb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [commits war crimes](https://substack.com/redirect/6eac99bc-4404-45c0-884b-7fe42d6d4d42?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and shares none of our values. A country that has profited off of us so enormously that they are attempting to build a $9 trillion hundred mile long skyscraper, taller than the empire state building, across their desert and, true to form, [using slave labor and killing anyone who stands in their way](https://substack.com/redirect/46000515-6b13-45d2-9631-e87ceba3fe0b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Taking it back to oil: The cost of rapidly decarbonizing the US power grid [is estimated at about $4.5 trillion](https://substack.com/redirect/e6ffdd9a-95cf-4c9c-bab1-3d0598873baa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), plus some amount for accelerating the transition to electric vehicles. That's a THIRD of what we spent in Iraq and Afghanistan. And a much better investment than what we get for the money we spend helping secure China's oil supply chain and continuing to screw around in the middle east.
For our national security, we must adopt an industrial policy to rapidly and fully decarbonize our energy and transportation sectors. We need to ditch all fossil fuels, not just oil, the same way we ditched whale oil for petroleum in the late 1800s.
There are people working hard to hide the fact, but our government knows it to be true.
The Pentagon has periodically issued reports on it for years. The Bush administration had one [in 2003](https://substack.com/redirect/4767066a-54ca-4e40-b845-b9c8a9d7a0cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the Obama administration [in 2014](https://substack.com/redirect/e264325d-300b-4e78-85ed-76b004e00fcc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [2015](https://substack.com/redirect/e5751289-1ac7-4b1b-acf2-6df4675be1d9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and, yes, even the Trump administration [in 2019](https://substack.com/redirect/ad3dc959-d0cd-45b2-a064-fd713b2ff696?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Throughout the years, the story hasn't changed. Climate change-induced severe weather has caused billions of dollars of damage to defense infrastructure and threatens to damage the majority of Defense Department installations, costing taxpayers billions over the long haul. Sea-level rise is undermining our naval bases. Climate change taxes the military by increasing refugee flows and other events that require humanitarian relief missions. It fuels conflicts over basic resources such as food and water that create instability and lead to military intervention.
An [increase in temperature directly correlates with an increase in violence](https://substack.com/redirect/42dbf2ea-943b-4aec-981c-66025241434d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
A recent study of conflict in Africa found that a 1 percent increase in temperature leads to a 4.5 percent increase in civil war in the same year and a 0.9 percent increase in the following year. By the year 2030, based on averaged data from 18 climate models, this would lead to a 54 percent increase in armed conflict on the continent over the next decade.
As defense budgets continue to rise, avoiding and preventing conflict is one of the best cost-saving measures available to U.S. policymakers and illustrates one of the key external benefits of decarbonization and slowing global warming. We can't go back and get the $14 trillion we spent over the last 20 years in the Middle East, but we can prevent the need (or desire) to spend the next $14 trillion by both making the region and its natural resources irrelevant and slowing global warming and the attendant increase of global violence.
This is a critical component to transitioning from the so-called war on terror and its regional power struggles to a focus on near-peer competitors like China and Russia and retaining the position of the West, and democratic values, in the international order.
As I've mentioned before, Russia's economy is [largely based around oil and gas](https://substack.com/redirect/136ecea8-2a93-473d-a385-b7bb26467348?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The war in Ukraine wouldn't even be happening if there was no market for Russia's oil and gas.
Instead of abandoning NATO, we should be using the leverage we have to bake decarbonization tech funding into our national-security agreements with Europe. Continue to provide our nuclear umbrella, that we already pay for, but give them a cheaper alternative to building their own by having them invest in NextGen energy development that they then buy from us, instead of Russian gas. The alternative is that Europe re-militarizes, more countries have nuclear weapons, and Europe ditches Russian gas and turns to China for the next generation of energy because we never built it out.
Terrible.
Especially considering that the technology we develop and know-how we build in the decarbonization process will be a boon to U.S. business as we export our knowledge, technology, and products overseas.
Crushing Russia and China and exporting U.S. tech and products at the same time? That's decarbonization.
Decarbonizing isn't even complicated. We have many of the tools to start doing it right now: the technology, the manpower, and the financial structure. The only thing missing is leadership with the courage to enact an industrial policy around decarbonization and the creativity to make it happen.
It could be funded by re-prioritizing the [$650 billion in fossil fuel subsidies](https://substack.com/redirect/9af61ba7-01f5-4146-9477-6d8a5f3b62be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the U.S. hands out each year. It could be funded by eliminating tax cuts. It could even be funded without spending a dime by transitioning the Federal Reserve from quantitative tightening back to quantitative easing-- but instead of focusing on [juicing the stock market](https://substack.com/redirect/315af03f-87e5-467c-9e1e-faa1dc707983?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and buying [fossil fuel junk bonds](https://substack.com/redirect/b3d92734-93db-4d41-a2a8-21335edda45c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), it could fund decarbonization. The federal government could back decarbonization-related loans, like federally backed mortgages, that the Federal Reserve would then purchase. Or we could create a special purpose vehicle with the Departments of Defense and Treasury for the purchase of green assets. It wouldn't even increase the Fed's $7 trillion balance sheet if done the right way.
There is precedent for this.
After World War II, the Department of Defense shaped industrial policy and the direction our country was headed. The department could [lead with its budget](https://substack.com/redirect/31ccd749-8526-4506-8d66-5df87f9d4d90?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in that regard. Other national security-based tools, like the Defense Production Act, which has numerous authorities and even energy-related sections, including an exhortation to [maximize renewable energy](https://substack.com/redirect/be61857e-3b0b-4cf1-bf15-c12ea8742b5f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), could also be leveraged.
The technical details can be accomplished in any number of ways. It just needs to get done. Building renewables here is like creating our own reservoirs of oil out of thin air.
In the end, this type of leadership shouldn't even be that risky.
I think leaders would find that if you offer the American people two worlds: one with more conflict, higher future defense spending, and a reliance on China; or one with more security, less military action, cleaner rivers and skies, and more economic opportunity, they would overwhelmingly pick the second.
Lucas

clean_body: The National Security Case for the Next Generation of Energy | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? The National Security Case for the Next Generation of Energy | | | Mar 15 | | | | | | | | | | | | Looks like someone high up read ! Because the US thankfully reversed course on the critical negotiating error of unilaterally giving up leverage like aid and intel sharing when dealing Russian realpolitik. Another thing I talked about, however, that has not been addressed, is how Europe made this conflict possible by buying what it thought was "cheap" natural gas from Russia for years. Gas that seemed cheap because the sticker price didn't include other external costs, like the fact that the money was going to fuel Russia's war machine which Europe is now having to spend a lot of money counteracting. In the long run, that "cheap" natural gas is going to cost significantly more than if they had invested in that money in the next generation of energy. America has been making the same mistake for decades, thinking that fossil fuels are cheap because we only look at the sticker price. It's time all of us doubled down on making the case that decarbonization and the transition to the next generation of energy is the critical ingredient of future American security and strength. I first , this is how it's done. Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. I spent a decent chunk of 2009 rolling down the roads of western Iraq, working with Iraqi police, and trying not to get blown up. A few years ago, on my birthday, I tried to reflect back on the birthday I spent there near the end of that deployment. How different it was. But, looking back, I couldn't even tell you where I went that day. Fallujah? Ramadi? Habbaniyah? It's hard to tell. It's been more than a decade since most of us were there and, while some things are still quite sharp, the day-to-day stuff is getting fuzzy. I did remember a running joke that would come up now and again, though, because it was exactly the type of thing that would come up on a birthday in Iraq. Occasionally, when the opportunity seemed right, someone would wonder aloud, "Why are we here again?" And the answer, delivered dryly, was always some refrain of: "Oh, yeah. Oil." It's not like we were being clever. Everyone knew it. The old CENTCOM commander, John Abizaid, had said it quite clearly in 2007: "Of course it's about oil; we can't really deny that." Alan Greenspan had agreed, and Chuck Hagel directly confirmed it: "People say we're not fighting for oil. Of course we are." It's the reason we've cared about the Middle East for decades. It's almost certainly the reason I spent parts of 2012, 2013, and 2014 in Afghanistan, too. Al-Qaeda would have likely never existed were it not for the U.S. obsession with oil, considering that the that al-Qaeda exploited centered around our actions in the Middle East. Without al-Qaeda, we would have cared about Afghanistan and the Taliban about the same amount we care about Eritrea and its repressive king. Which, of course, is not at all. Without an oil addiction, the U.S. military could avoid a number of costly and resource-intensive still-ongoing operations in the area, such as working the Fifth Fleet overtime to keep the open. In 2019, Secretary of State Mike Pompeo affirmed that the U.S. would keep the straits , and that policy has never wavered, regardless of the President. Which is an incredible amount of dedication for an expensive policy that, ironically, helps our adversary, China, more than us. , not America. And China received more than double the amount that came here. As my generation enters middle age and we look back at the several decades of conflict we endured because of oil, and the effort we continue to put toward it, it's obvious that it has never been worth the cost. The time. The lives. The dollars. The price tag on U.S. actions in Iraq, Afghanistan, Pakistan, and Syria in the 21st century totals . Not to mention the intangible cost of allying with countries like Saudi Arabia, incubator of 15 of the 19 9/11 hijackers. The country that and anti-American sentiment for decades. The repressive country in the world that , , and shares none of our values. A country that has profited off of us so enormously that they are attempting to build a $9 trillion hundred mile long skyscraper, taller than the empire state building, across their desert and, true to form, . Taking it back to oil: The cost of rapidly decarbonizing the US power grid , plus some amount for accelerating the transition to electric vehicles. That's a THIRD of what we spent in Iraq and Afghanistan. And a much better investment than what we get for the money we spend helping secure China's oil supply chain and continuing to screw around in the middle east. For our national security, we must adopt an industrial policy to rapidly and fully decarbonize our energy and transportation sectors. We need to ditch all fossil fuels, not just oil, the same way we ditched whale oil for petroleum in the late 1800s. There are people working hard to hide the fact, but our government knows it to be true. The Pentagon has periodically issued reports on it for years. The Bush administration had one , the Obama administration and , and, yes, even the Trump administration . Throughout the years, the story hasn't changed. Climate change-induced severe weather has caused billions of dollars of damage to defense infrastructure and threatens to damage the majority of Defense Department installations, costing taxpayers billions over the long haul. Sea-level rise is undermining our naval bases. Climate change taxes the military by increasing refugee flows and other events that require humanitarian relief missions. It fuels conflicts over basic resources such as food and water that create instability and lead to military intervention. An . A recent study of conflict in Africa found that a 1 percent increase in temperature leads to a 4.5 percent increase in civil war in the same year and a 0.9 percent increase in the following year. By the year 2030, based on averaged data from 18 climate models, this would lead to a 54 percent increase in armed conflict on the continent over the next decade. As defense budgets continue to rise, avoiding and preventing conflict is one of the best cost-saving measures available to U.S. policymakers and illustrates one of the key external benefits of decarbonization and slowing global warming. We can't go back and get the $14 trillion we spent over the last 20 years in the Middle East, but we can prevent the need (or desire) to spend the next $14 trillion by both making the region and its natural resources irrelevant and slowing global warming and the attendant increase of global violence. This is a critical component to transitioning from the so-called war on terror and its regional power struggles to a focus on near-peer competitors like China and Russia and retaining the position of the West, and democratic values, in the international order. As I've mentioned before, Russia's economy is . The war in Ukraine wouldn't even be happening if there was no market for Russia's oil and gas. Instead of abandoning NATO, we should be using the leverage we have to bake decarbonization tech funding into our national-security agreements with Europe. Continue to provide our nuclear umbrella, that we already pay for, but give them a cheaper alternative to building their own by having them invest in NextGen energy development that they then buy from us, instead of Russian gas. The alternative is that Europe re-militarizes, more countries have nuclear weapons, and Europe ditches Russian gas and turns to China for the next generation of energy because we never built it out. Terrible. Especially considering that the technology we develop and know-how we build in the decarbonization process will be a boon to U.S. business as we export our knowledge, technology, and products overseas. Crushing Russia and China and exporting U.S. tech and products at the same time? That's decarbonization. Decarbonizing isn't even complicated. We have many of the tools to start doing it right now: the technology, the manpower, and the financial structure. The only thing missing is leadership with the courage to enact an industrial policy around decarbonization and the creativity to make it happen. It could be funded by re-prioritizing the the U.S. hands out each year. It could be funded by eliminating tax cuts. It could even be funded without spending a dime by transitioning the Federal Reserve from quantitative tightening back to quantitative easing-- but instead of focusing on and buying , it could fund decarbonization. The federal government could back decarbonization-related loans, like federally backed mortgages, that the Federal Reserve would then purchase. Or we could create a special purpose vehicle with the Departments of Defense and Treasury for the purchase of green assets. It wouldn't even increase the Fed's $7 trillion balance sheet if done the right way. There is precedent for this. After World War II, the Department of Defense shaped industrial policy and the direction our country was headed. The department could in that regard. Other national security-based tools, like the Defense Production Act, which has numerous authorities and even energy-related sections, including an exhortation to , could also be leveraged. The technical details can be accomplished in any number of ways. It just needs to get done. Building renewables here is like creating our own reservoirs of oil out of thin air. In the end, this type of leadership shouldn't even be that risky. I think leaders would find that if you offer the American people two worlds: one with more conflict, higher future defense spending, and a reliance on China; or one with more security, less military action, cleaner rivers and skies, and more economic opportunity, they would overwhelmingly pick the second. Military supporters like you who gave in honor of K-9 Veterans Day helped ensure that our heroic K-9 teams and service members around the world will get the resources and support they deserve this year and into the future.
[**THANK YOU!**](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/a9zxSPLA-YC_/SXorS1o0ZEIzS2dBanpFMGplZGxEQ3BHbno1SFhuK0NQdEpTOXBVMUd6ekQzNXdlUkxoNzBNSWQ4Yk5wMWVsNGhDUG85SytUUklwK01zTUVQN0U2VWpLT0xLUWlKcnBycFdLM3pqR0xONUU9S0/L29yTE52Tlk4U1VhYjFoMUhVbW5xaDFndTkrUDFzVlEwNHViVmUweWRtai9Oc3lkWGNLMXdvSkZTOTdRQStQRE8rWXBEMTVvQ0JqaUdyVnNPUldPWXA2bGprV0l5TlZnS0)

We're so proud of the impact we've been able to make together, and we can't thank you enough for bringing the USO's mission to life by standing with us, our military working dogs and their handlers and all our military heroes.
Thank you again,
The USO
P.S. In case you didn't get a chance to donate for K-9 Veterans Day, we have great news! **You can still make a gift now to support our K-9 teams and all our service members:[Will you make a gift now to help us continue supporting military K-9 teams and all our brave heroes around the world?*](https://links.e.uso.)**

clean_body: The USO and all our military heroes are so grateful to you Thank you. Military supporters like you who gave in honor of K-9 Veterans Day helped ensure that our heroic K-9 teams and service members around the world will get the resources and support they deserve this year and into the future. We're so proud of the impact we've been able to make together, and we can't thank you enough for bringing the USO's mission to life by standing with us, our military working dogs and their handlers and all our military heroes. Thank you again, The USO P.S. In case you didn't get a chance to donate for K-9 Veterans Day, we have great news! You can still make a gift now to support our K-9 teams and all our service members: *Your contribution will be joined with the contributions of others and used where it is needed most to strengthen the well-being of the people serving in America's military and their families. United Service Organizations, Inc. (USO) is a nonprofit 501(ς)(3) organization (EIN/Tax ID: 13-1610451) and not part of the Department of Defense (DOD). name: Sen. Jacky Rosen
subject: 3 things I need you to know
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rosen for Nevada
body:
I'm fighting as hard as I can for you and your family.
| | |
---
Friend, ahead of tonight's mid-month fundraising deadline at midnight, **there are three things I need you to know:**
**1)** I'm fighting with everything I have alongside Senate Democrats to stop the GOP from inflicting more harm on our families. That includes standing against Republican plans to cut Medicaid so they can pay for tax breaks for their billionaire friends – and demanding Elon Musk back off on his plans to gut Social Security.
**2)** If we're going to truly put a stop to the Trump administration's cruelty, corruption, and recklessness, we need to take back the Senate.
**3)** Our grassroots fundraising is more important than ever – because Musk and Trump's other billionaire friends are ready to spend whatever it takes to tip the scales in Republicans' favor.
**But with time running out before tonight's deadline, we're still $3,189 short of our mid-month goal. Each and every dollar is pivotal to laying the groundwork for Democrats to retake the Senate majority and protect the things we care about most – from Medicaid and Social Security to cancer research, veterans benefits, and meal programs for seniors.**
**[_So please, friend, can I count on you to chip in $5, $25, or anything you can spare before midnight? Your grassroots support will directly fuel our efforts to flip the Senate blue and stop Donald Trump and Elon Musk._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103916066/533667356/-105628576?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&refcode=jtk5490-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNTkwODkzYi1iNzAxLWYwMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4rohvQuBzIrItjslBwNiF7LNjF8y_rTtgVHXZ7s6IPw=)**

clean_body: I'm fighting as hard as I can for you and your family. | | | Friend, ahead of tonight's mid-month fundraising deadline at midnight, there are three things I need you to know: 1) I'm fighting with everything I have alongside Senate Democrats to stop the GOP from inflicting more harm on our families. That includes standing against Republican plans to cut Medicaid so they can pay for tax breaks for their billionaire friends – and demanding Elon Musk back off on his plans to gut Social Security. 2) If we're going to truly put a stop to the Trump administration's cruelty, corruption, and recklessness, we need to take back the Senate. 3) Our grassroots fundraising is more important than ever – because Musk and Trump's other billionaire friends are ready to spend whatever it takes to tip the scales in Republicans' favor. But with time running out before tonight's deadline, we're still $3,189 short of our mid-month goal. Each and every dollar is pivotal to laying the groundwork for Democrats to retake the Senate majority and protect the things we care about most – from Medicaid and Social Security to cancer research, veterans benefits, and meal programs for seniors. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | | = | | = | | = It means the world to have you by my side in the fight ahead. name: Marine Winsome Earle-Sears
subject: Semper Fi. Always faithful.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Winsome for Governor
body:
I’ve lived a full, rewarding life – and I owe it all to this country. In a world of so much uncertainty, I know one thing to be 100% true: my story is only possible in America.
I often get asked, "Were you really willing to put your life on the line for a country you weren't even born in?" And to that, I say absolutely.
| |

**Semper Fi._Always faithful._** I often get asked, _" Were you really willing to put your life on the line for a country you weren't even born in?"_
---
[](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=6de9dbb3ac85483f901ff837e83c6566&_e=EisxLjN_kZSpxlH7FpcJCpb-w4h0fuo1Mmhwd6fDkU-ykna2XdIAqQaKm1MWWBzwKPuFMmebhG4nMCUAiO2A-QFjwyF_J1Le38m4WKXoox3QBkzo3BBW6fwCQtHXfRqWkVpO6IhcWl4BaYbdOx4e-eTm0Ngg45GlpBDNVp_NWRN87pMQWCyTpcUON1VwuNoLEofgB_Kxldhwa72oV0v-n6UGNdXD-mxmnnBwwX53wfNseIv_46zfzqVXPYgmjooGZBLtpyx_MMfxePeqQx8qA0eiXLvqkjvuUPZvifnFNQzby8j-xNT6DoON8gR1ONoqv0bkE-MoukNN72VmB4PiRwut1_rqqS8Qt447pPgstdOJyVbQ2_crayUYxYMp5lrAr7_hrZpGvfoQs7DRYThXEQ%3D%3D)

And to that, I say **absolutely.** Friend, my name is Winsome Earle-Sears. I don't often make promises, but I can promise you this: I'm not your typical politician, **and I 'm not like the others who normally send you these messages:**-I immigrated to the US from Jamaica with my family when I was six years old.
-I served in the Marine Corps as an electrician.
-I ran a women's homeless shelter and a men's prison ministry.I've lived a full, rewarding life - and I owe it all to this country. In a world of so much uncertainty, I know one thing to be 100% true: _**my story is only possible in America.**_ And now, I'm running for Governor of Virginia - to make certain that the same country that let a poor, black woman ascend to the highest levels of politics remains that way - a beacon of hope.

_But the reality we have to confront is that those on the Left are actively trying to tear this down. They aim to destroy the middle class, strip away our 2nd Amendment rights, and brainwash our children._**Not on my watch. ** _**[So, consider this a humble invitation to join my campaign: Will you chip in today--even if it's just a few bucks--to ensure that we have the means needed to beat my Democrat opponent and win this thing?](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=6de9dbb3ac85483f901ff837e83c6566&_e=EisxLjN_kZSpxlH7FpcJCpb-w4h0fuo1Mmhwd6fDkU-ykna2XdIAqQaKm1MWWBzwKPuFMmebhG4nMCUAiO2A-QFjwyF_J1Le38m4WKXoox3QBkzo3BBW6fwCQtHXfRqWkVpO6IhcWl4BaYbdOx4e-eTm0Ngg45GlpBDNVp_NWRN87pMQWCyTpcUON1VwuNoLEofgB_Kxldhwa72oV0v-n6UGNdXD-mxmnnBwwX53wfNseIv_46zfzqVXPYgmjooGlgL5l2ehQ-6TfK2ualPYcfRazNEw2SoUN7yGa0AsOBasthX5UPn9Yz6xs_UGss1SYyhKcvAI8AmXAp287Olu6mptXzRAYnRH7ZoaQTAuaJEjQwvrjd3wkL2gXEVTny_rezvMpPW4atMg526_kjJGEg%3D%3D)**_

clean_body: I’ve lived a full, rewarding life – and I owe it all to this country. In a world of so much uncertainty, I know one thing to be 100% true: my story is only possible in America. I often get asked, "Were you really willing to put your life on the line for a country you weren't even born in?" And to that, I say absolutely. | | | | | | | | | Semper Fi.Always faithful. I often get asked, " Were you really willing to put your life on the line for a country you weren't even born in?" And to that, I say absolutely. Friend, my name is Winsome Earle-Sears. I don't often make promises, but I can promise you this: I'm not your typical politician, and I 'm not like the others who normally send you these messages:-I immigrated to the US from Jamaica with my family when I was six years old. -I served in the Marine Corps as an electrician. -I ran a women's homeless shelter and a men's prison ministry.I've lived a full, rewarding life - and I owe it all to this country. In a world of so much uncertainty, I know one thing to be 100% true: my story is only possible in America. And now, I'm running for Governor of Virginia - to make certain that the same country that let a poor, black woman ascend to the highest levels of politics remains that way - a beacon of hope. But the reality we have to confront is that those on the Left are actively trying to tear this down. They aim to destroy the middle class, strip away our 2nd Amendment rights, and brainwash our children.Not on my watch. We have a long journey ahead of us, but we will be better off with you on our side. Thank you & God Bless, Winsome Earle-Sears Candidate for Governor of Virginia | I'm Winsome Earle-Sears, Republican candidate running for Governor of Virginia. I broke records as the first black female Lieutenant Governor in Virginia, but my mission isn't over yet! It's going to all of us conservatives working together to win. | | Paid for by Winsome for Governor PO Box 71596 Richmond, VA 23255-1596 Military disclaimer: Neither military info nor photographs of Winsome Earle-Sears in uniform imply endorsement of Winsome Earle-Sears by the Department of Defense or its particular military departments.

### So, Mahmoud, here's the deal: you don't like Israel, you don't like America, you don't like the West? Fine. There's a plane leaving tonight--get on it.
Mar 15
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3072780&post_id=159129088&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU5MTI5MDg4LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0MjA1NjMxOCwiZXhwIjoxNzQ0NjQ4MzE4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzA3Mjc4MCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Vqvlx1v67RF-oNlml8RnF_95d9lOXo4f75MVtMEqMs0&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/166a265e-9b81-47c6-801e-d7552026b5a7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**By Rudy Giuliani**
Listen up, folks--America is under attack, not just from abroad but from within, and it's time we take a stand. The case of Mahmoud Khalil, a Columbia University student and anti-Israel agitator, is a glaring example of what's wrong with letting radicals infiltrate our country and our elite institutions. This guy's not here to contribute to the American dream--he's here to tear it down, and he's gotta go. President Trump's got it right: deporting Khalil is the first step in rooting out pervasive anti-Semitism and protecting our values.
Let's get one thing straight: Israel is our ally, one of the strongest we've got. They're a democracy in a sea of chaos, standing shoulder-to-shoulder with us against terrorism and tyranny. Our government's support for Israel isn't up for debate--it's a bedrock of our foreign policy, forged through decades of shared struggle and mutual respect. If your whole purpose for being here is to protest that alliance, to undermine it with hate and lies, then you don't belong in the United States. Pack your bags, Mahmoud--Syria's waiting.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..ao2VJE0JS_-VHAZ0k6byH1QSeT-MnIiyoVCWTXX9s2s?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=159129088)
Now, let's talk facts. Khalil wasn't just some kid with a sign at a rally. This guy was a ringleader, a graduate student at Columbia who turned that campus into a cesspool of anti-Israel venom. He distributed propaganda--right there on American soil--celebrating the October 7th attacks, that barbaric assault by Hamas that slaughtered over 1,100 innocent people, including women and children. Those flyers didn't just cheer the violence; they featured and praised Yahya Sinwar, the head honcho of Hamas, a group the U.S. has designated a terrorist organization since 1997. That's not free speech--that's propaganda for murderers. And it gets worse: Khalil's rhetoric dripped with anti-Semitic bile, the kind of hate that's fueled pogroms and genocide. This isn't about "criticizing policy"--it's about vilifying Jews and glorifying terror.
_**Now, a brief message from our sponsor (piece continues below)**_
* * *
**[Presented By AmmoSquared](https://substack.com/redirect/bbd0f300-19a4-427e-b64e-31589931d82b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# Hope Won't Fill Your Magazines...
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/bbd0f300-19a4-427e-b64e-31589931d82b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Remember the ammo shortage of 2020? Empty shelves, sold-out stock--it was a nightmare. What if you never had to worry about that again?
Introducing **[AmmoSquared, America's Ammunition Reserve](https://substack.com/redirect/bbd0f300-19a4-427e-b64e-31589931d82b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[.](https://substack.com/redirect/bbd0f300-19a4-427e-b64e-31589931d82b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Since 2015, nearly 100,000 members have built personal ammo stockpiles--automatically and effortlessly.
**How it works:**
* **Sign up** - Choose from 70+ calibers.
* **Set your budget** - Automate your ammo purchases.
* **Pick shipping frequency** - Get ammo when you need it.
* **We store it** - Secure, climate-controlled warehouses.

No minimums. No hidden fees. Just peace of mind.
**Special Offer:** Sign up today and get **75 rounds of 9mm practice ammo** to jumpstart your reserve.
Don't wait for the next shortage--because hope won't fill your magazines. **[Click here to get started!](https://substack.com/redirect/bbd0f300-19a4-427e-b64e-31589931d82b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[ Shop Ammo Now](https://substack.com/redirect/bbd0f300-19a4-427e-b64e-31589931d82b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
_**(Continued)**_
The Trump administration's response? Spot on. The President's recent executive order, cracking down on this garbage, is aligned with federal law--specifically, **[the Immigration and Nationality Act](https://substack.com/redirect/408e01e2-d5db-47fa-b703-262e2b9dff63?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , which lets us boot out anyone providing material support or endorsement to designated terrorist outfits like Hamas. Khalil's actions fit the bill. He's not just some protester with a megaphone like the media has painted him to be; he's an organizer for a group that's sworn to wipe Israel off the map and kill Americans while they're at it. The Department of Homeland Security says he "led activities aligned to Hamas"--that's enough for me, and it should be enough for any red-blooded American. Trump's making it clear: we won't coddle terrorists or their fanboys. Khalil's arrest is the first of many, and I say, "Good riddance."
Let's cut through the noise: Khalil's a radical Islamist who hates Western values. His group at Columbia, the so-called Columbia University Apartheid Divest, didn't just call for divestment from Israel--they've got a manifesto that screams for the total eradication of Western civilization. This isn't just about Palestine--it's about tearing down everything America and the West stands for: freedom, democracy, our Judeo-Christian roots. Khalil grew up in a Palestinian refugee camp in Syria, and instead of embracing the opportunity America gave him--a green card, a spot at an Ivy League school--he spits in our face. He's not here to build; he's here to destroy.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9a89b221-899b-42a4-88ca-d513ae02e171?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
We can't let this slide. If the United States is going to stand tall, we can't open our doors to people who despise us. Immigration isn't a free-for-all--it's a privilege, and it comes with a responsibility to respect this country, not undermine it. Our elite universities shouldn't be playgrounds for radicals who hate America and our allies. Columbia's already lost $400 million in federal funding for letting this anti-Semitism fester--good. Maybe now they'll think twice before coddling the next Khalil.
I've spent my life fighting for justice, from locking up mobsters as a prosecutor to leading New York through 9/11. I know a bad actor when I see one, and Khalil's it. He's not some misunderstood activist--he's a Trojan horse for ideologies that clash with everything we hold dear. The First Amendment doesn't protect shouting "fire" in a crowded theater, and it sure as hell doesn't protect boosting terrorists on our dime. Trump's executive order is a wake-up call: we're done tolerating this nonsense.
So, Mahmoud, here's the deal: you don't like Israel, you don't like America, you don't like the West? Fine. There's a plane leaving tonight--get on it. We're not here to babysit your hate. America's too great, too strong, to let radicals like you take up space. Deport him now, and let's keep America safe, free, and proud.
* * *

name: Magaziner HQ
subject: Seth is trying to reach you!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY Magaziner for Congress

**That's a significant amount of money, making me the Democrats' #1 target going forward. Now, I'm up for re-election, and the Left is ready to put up a serious challenge against me.**

name: Team Bonta
subject: Good news →
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642
body:
A judge ordered Trump to rehire thousands of federal employees because of Rob’s lawsuit, but this fight isn’t over.
# [](https://act.robbonta.com/go/3?refcode=em20250315&t=1&akid=1694%2E1088799%2EgHWtcq)
Some good news: A federal judge ruled that the Trump administration must rehire thousands of illegally fired federal workers because of Rob’s lawsuit.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250315?refcode=em20250315-1694&refcode2=1694_1088799_gHWtcq&akid=1694%2E1088799%2EgHWtcq)
**This is a huge victory for California and the nation.** Every day, these dedicated employees provide critical services and support to people like our veterans and farmers, steward our public lands, and ensure the government works for everyday families.
It just goes to show: _We can defeat Trump’s harmful and unlawful agenda when we stand with leaders like Rob and fight back._
While we may have won this fight for now, there will be many more as Trump continues to sow chaos in the federal government and hurt working families.
**[Make no mistake, Rob will continue to fight for Californians and Americans across the country as long as this administration violates the law. But he can’t do it alone — can you pitch in $5 to fund our campaign?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250315?refcode=em20250315-1694&amount=5&refcode2=1694_1088799_gHWtcq&akid=1694%2E1088799%2EgHWtcq)**

Thanks for all you do,
Team Bonta

name: VoteVets
subject: VoteVets is defeating Trump and Elon in court.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
A federal judge just told the Trump administration that their mass firings of probationary federal employees — including thousands of Veterans — were illegal.
#
Peter:
Don't buy into the idea that Trump and Elon are invincible, or inevitable.
We’re talking about a creepy, weird, fascist man-child and a 78 year old who eats nothing but well done steak, chicken nuggets, and diet soda. Frankly, neither of them are all that smart.
We can outlast these guys — and we can beat them. Matter of fact, we just did.
A federal judge just told the Trump administration that their mass firings of probationary federal employees — including thousands of Veterans — were illegal. He ordered the federal agencies responsible to reinstate the folks they fired to their jobs immediately. VoteVets was a plaintiff in this case, alongside the American Federation of Government Employees and other allies of ours.
This bears repeating: **We won.** This is a good thing.
It’s worth taking a minute to celebrate that, especially with all the bad news we’re enduring day after day. And it’s a really good example of what we do with the donations you send our way.
**When you donate to VoteVets you fund our work on cases like these.** We’re involved in several more, and each are critical checks on Trump and Elon’s power. The fight is far from over, and the work continues every day.
[**If supporting lawsuits like this one sounds like a worthy use of $5 or $10, please: Chip in and help us keep up the fight.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-mar25.WvYIqH&refcode=em250315-1-90&t=1&akid=34251%2E3013755%2E0eiehd)

| Team,

Last month, I did something I don’t normally do: a “stitch” on TikTok replying to someone expressing despair about Trump’s attacks on the foundations of our democracy.

I am a proud digitally-challenged Millennial, and not especially skilled at social media influencing or content creation. When I jumped into this conversation, it felt unnatural and a little awkward. (_Did I mention that I’m a Millennial?_) **
But to be honest, I am just upset.** I’m frustrated, scared, and feeling deeply uncertain about _basic things that until recently we could take for granted_ like the ability to control my own body, to make my own choice about starting a family (or not), children having protection from preventable and devastating diseases like measles and polio, and being free to protest and disagree politically without the threat of violence or retribution...to name just a few.

So when I started sharing my thoughts online, I was a bit out of my comfort zone — but many others share these feelings, and it touched a nerve.

name: Sarah McBride
subject: Republicans are a bunch of overgrown schoolyard bullies
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic Party of Georgia
body:
Our constituents shouldn’t have to suffer because Republicans want to act like children.

If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/502xl98alkm3J5MS-c04xA2?unsubscribedata=FbP01ou%2BlNGp1TXa9vKg8ZTgx8rNtAZm8ZVtmVqMEf%2BN%2BDM4L1khirejiKFpT82ZWFRi0R6rI3whlNj7T%2F4x9ekv7WNYGrKtRKeVAXs0MVZH8rZnIf7KwO5EnNoO%2FOhq6SsEBeW33g1rtXOMyDEMXI1RtLjDu6kdLo6u%2FSEAliQU2VRmZnZDOKYx%2FWRj5a237DVEwt1tWakR3HY8XpAktwenugUv9%2FbA31xLexkHolpYNmy8BgmmH1zri8ggWq1UNzCcwihXikegz1x4dMJkv%2BLuUuX7V%2BnXHJbsJqP%2BABGHQY1VXzL8vLrGvxDzD8AqaJdySNE8AimVLKTM%2BMYk1cf9yZK4DsI9hjpDLVVqt0o8IHRLGc%2By2HDqsdOVXrDxA0H29SzlcTOvrWpfszlgDQJt2Wwnc%2BuwescbHKNy4i2nB9qL09bsJLn7S8DPQokZ)
---
| Hi Peter, it’s Congresswoman Sarah McBride. The Democratic Party of Georgia said I could reach out because you have what it takes to stand up in critical moments — and this is one of them. **Yet another GOP politician decided to get his own 15 Minutes of Twitter Fame at my expense.** At a subcommittee hearing, the Republican chairman referred to me as “Mr. McBride.” And when Rep. Bill Keating defended me, the chair _adjourned the hearing_ to avoid acknowledging his own immaturity. **Republicans are a bunch of overgrown schoolyard bullies.** Our constituents shouldn’t have to suffer because Republicans want to act like children. **This pettiness needs to end.** I was there ready to ask questions on nuclear non-proliferation and US support for our democratic allies in Europe. **I ran for Congress to roll up my sleeves and do the job.** No matter what they throw at me, I’m 100% focused on delivering for Delawareans. And if that means listening to their childish insults, _so be it._ [Peter, I’m proud to have the Democratic Party of Georgia’s support at this moment, but we need you with us. The Democratic Party of Georgia said I could reach out because you have what it takes to stand up in critical moments — and this is one of them. Yet another GOP politician decided to get his own 15 Minutes of Twitter Fame at my expense. At a subcommittee hearing, the Republican chairman referred to me as “Mr. McBride.” And when Rep. Bill Keating defended me, the chair adjourned the hearing to avoid acknowledging his own immaturity. Republicans are a bunch of overgrown schoolyard bullies. Our constituents shouldn’t have to suffer because Republicans want to act like children. This pettiness needs to end. I was there ready to ask questions on nuclear non-proliferation and US support for our democratic allies in Europe. I ran for Congress to roll up my sleeves and do the job. No matter what they throw at me, I’m 100% focused on delivering for Delawareans. clean_body: | = = Peter, Before I explain, will you = toward our first end-of-quarter goal of the year? The end of the month marks the first FEC deadline since I was sworn into Congress, and a strong showing will set the tone. I’ve only been in Congress for two months, and Republicans have already singled me out as a top target. Attacks are increasing by the day – we just received word that the NRCC is funding a digital ad against me. As one of the only Democrats to flip a red seat in November, the GOP knows this seat is key to House control. It’s why national Democrats named CA-27 a “Frontline” race – their program focused on protecting the most vulnerable Democrats in Congress. Defending this seat gives Democrats the best chance of holding this administration accountable – and the work must start now. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | **= | = ---|--- = | = = | =** Thank you, George | = = = George has spent his career solving problems. name: Congresswoman Sarah Elfreth
subject: Standing up for our students and teachers!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Elfreth for Maryland
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/blu-sefc-q1-2025-em?refcode=BLU_EM_SEFC_2025-01-04_1&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/blu-sefc-q1-2025-em?refcode=BLU_EM_SEFC_2025-01-04_1&amount=25]
I wouldn’t be where I am today without my public school education. And while we knew that Project 2025 called for the dismantling of the Department of Education, it has been appalling to witness this week’s round of mass layoffs that will only lead to a less just, a less efficient, and a less successful education system for millions of American students.
Far from the surgical approach described by President Trump, the Department of Education has now lost an additional 1,300 employees—leaving them with nearly half their original staff. Specifically, the Administration has targeted the Civil Rights Department, which makes sure students, including students with disabilities, aren’t being discriminated against. This Administration knows it would take an act of Congress to dissolve the Department—that’s why they are dismantling it civil servant by civil servant instead.
Let’s be clear about what this means: the Trump Administration is going after working families and our most vulnerable students.
In response to these attacks, I’ve demanded answers from the Secretary of Education as to how full funding and services will remain available in spite of the devastating staff cuts. I’ve also introduced legislation that ensures probationary employees—the first to be cut from the DOE—are allowed to continue their work should they be reinstated.
I’m here to stand with our students, public school families, and educators, but I need your help. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/blu-sefc-q2-2025-em?refcode=blu_em_sefc_2025-03-15]

Thank you,
Sarah Elfreth

Peter,
We just wrapped up another incredible town hall in Omaha, and I couldn’t be more fired up.

Elon Musk and Donald Trump are going to continue to go after our Social Security. Trump’s tariffs are going to continue to raise prices, hurting you, your families and small businesses. And they’re going to continue to try to give huge tax cuts to billionaires on top of it all.

[Can you pitch in $5 now to help them stop extremism and keep Minnesota in DFL hands?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103919789/533679700/-704849132?refcode=footer-body&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZDIwNjllOS1iZjAxLWYwMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4Y3VNgL9YX0CYBRQjRixJrCxnOxMEF07fZ_aP5gIOeg=) | **[DONATE NOW ➞](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103919791/533679702/-2074829820?refcode=footer-button&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZDIwNjllOS1iZjAxLWYwMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4Y3VNgL9YX0CYBRQjRixJrCxnOxMEF07fZ_aP5gIOeg=)**

I’m in this fight to get out there and tell the truth about what Republicans are doing and show the American people that Democrats are offering something better.
